name: LoanMax Title Loans
description_short: Located at 544 N Semoran Blvd, Orlando, FL 32807, LoanMax Title Loans can be reached at +1 407-436-7390.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia and has grown into one of America's largest title loan networks. The company has operated for over 25 years, positioning itself as a respected alternative lender for consumers seeking quick access to cash using vehicle equity. The Southgate, Michigan location at 12850 Eureka Road is one of their many franchised branches serving the Detroit metropolitan area.

LoanMax specializes exclusively in auto title loans, which allow borrowers to leverage the equity in their vehicles to access emergency cash up to $10,000. The application process requires only a vehicle, photo ID, and clear vehicle title. The company accepts multiple payment methods including cash, check, debit card, and Western Union transfers. Loans are subject to vehicle evaluation and ability to repay. The Southgate location operates Monday-Friday 10 AM-6 PM and Saturday 9 AM-2 PM, with additional locations in Taylor, Garden City, Detroit, and Center Line nearby.

LoanMax distinguishes itself through its extensive multi-state network, allowing customers to access services across numerous locations. The company maintains state licensing and regulatory compliance, with documented licenses for Michigan, Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, and Nevada. They provide state-specific consumer information and maximum rate schedules, demonstrating transparency regarding regulatory requirements. The company operates a straightforward approval process focused on vehicle collateral rather than credit history or income verification.

Title loans carry inherent risks that consumers should understand clearly. These are short-term, high-cost debt products secured by vehicle collateral—failure to repay risks vehicle repossession. While LoanMax markets loans up to $10,000, actual approval amounts depend on vehicle evaluation. The website lacks transparent APR disclosure, making true cost comparison difficult. These loans function best as genuine emergencies, not ongoing financial solutions. Nevada's consumer warnings against using title loans as long-term solutions reflect broader industry concerns about predatory lending patterns.
pros: Up to $10,000 available per loan, higher than typical payday loans
Multiple payment methods accepted (cash, check, debit card, Western Union)
Extensive multi-state network with 30+ locations for accessibility
State-licensed and regulated in Michigan and other jurisdictions with documented compliance
Quick same-day funding available for qualified applicants
No credit check requirement—approval based on vehicle value instead
Simple application requiring only vehicle, ID, and clear title
cons: Vehicle collateral at risk of repossession if loan defaults
APR rates not disclosed on website, making true cost comparison impossible
Short-term product with high interest rates typical of title loans
Loan approval and amounts subject to vehicle evaluation, not guaranteed
Can trap borrowers in debt cycle if used repeatedly for ongoing financial needs
best_for: Consumers with vehicle equity who need $1,000-$10,000 in emergency cash within 24 hours
Borrowers with poor or no credit history who cannot qualify for traditional loans
People facing unexpected expenses (medical, car repair, utility bills) and need immediate funding
diagnosis: LoanMax is appropriate for consumers experiencing genuine financial emergencies who own vehicles outright or have significant equity, need cash within 24 hours, and cannot qualify for traditional loans. Critical caveat: title loans are high-cost short-term products where vehicle repossession is the consequence of default; they should never be used as ongoing financial solutions or for non-essential purchases.
services: Multiple payment method acceptance (cash, check, debit card, Western Union)
Multi-location service with nearby branch options
FAQ resources on title loan process
Customer account management and login portal
